'''Kromol''' was the fifth [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Kro'eval system]]. It was largely uninhabited, with no [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species.

However, neighboring [[Kromus]], the fourth planet in the system, had 1.3 billion inhabitants, and was used by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] as the site of the [[Shadowblade|Starkiller]] superweapon facility during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

The information on Kromol and Kromus in [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] records was swapped, leading the Republic to believe that the Starkiller was being built on an uninhabited world. This error, probably manipulated by [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], led to the decision to plan the [[Battle of Kromus/Legends|Battle of Kromus]] around an orbital bombardment, and ultimately caused the wiping out of all life on Kromus.

==Behind the scenes==
Due to the information being swapped, is not entirely clear which are the correct names for the fourth and fifth planets in the Kro'eval system: the fourth planet is normally referred to as "Kromus", so this article assumes that "Kromol" is the correct name for the fifth planet; but [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Darrus Jeht/Legends|Darrus Jeht]] subsequently thinks of the destroyed fourth planet as "Kromol", which suggests the names might have been swapped around.
